intro: Declieuxia tenuiflora, also known as the "Brazilian ironwort," is a plant species native to Brazil, belonging to the Rubiaceae family. Traditionally, Declieuxia tenuiflora is used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including anemia, due to its reported ability to increase red blood cell count and iron levels in the body. The bioactive constituents of Declieuxia tenuiflora include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Declieuxia tenuiflora is often prepared as a tea or infusion, and it is also used in capsule or tablet form to support iron deficiency and anemia treatment.
